context_for_review: Diffusion Policy applies DDPM denoising to robot action trajectories conditioned on visual observations. Key advantage: handles multimodal action distributions unlike behavioral cloning. Architecture: 1D temporal CNN or transformer; action chunking predicts H future steps at once. 46.9% improvement over prior SOTA. At review: check for DP3 follow-ups, flow-matching variants (pi-zero), VLA integration, and any AWS/SageMaker robotics announcements.

Managed safety layer for Bedrock LLMs: content filters (Hate/Insults/Sexual/Violence/Misconduct/Prompt Attack), PII redaction, denied topics, hallucination grounding. Standard tier (June 2025) adds 60-language support, typo-resilient detection, code-embedded content detection, cross-region inference. Advanced tier adds granular denied-topic control. apply_guardrail() API allows standalone use without LLM invocation. At review: check for new filter categories, pricing changes, GA of new features, image filter adoption.

Probabilistic data structure: bit array of m bits + k hash functions. Guarantees no false negatives; false positives are tunable. FPR formula: p=(1-e^(-kn/m))^k; optimal k*=0.693*(m/n); at optimal k* the array is 50% full. Cannot delete elements (use Counting Bloom Filter instead). Memory efficient: 1M items at 1% FPR needs ~1.2MB vs 100MB+ for hash set. Used in Redis/ElastiCache (BF.ADD, BF.EXISTS), Cassandra SSTable lookups, Chrome malicious URL filtering, content dedup pipelines. At review time search: bloom filter Redis AWS new features, counting bloom filter vs cuckoo filter.

Unified model-agnostic API for all Bedrock models (claude, nova, mistral, titan). One message format works across all models; switch via modelId only. Supports multi-turn conversations (grow messages list), streaming via converse_stream, and tool use (function calling) via toolConfig. Payload limit ~3.75MB vs InvokeModel's 5MB. Key methods: converse(), converse_stream(). At review time: check which new models are supported, whether payload limits changed, and new features like document handling or vision input updates.

Binary hash trees: each node = SHA256 of its children, root = single tamper-evident dataset fingerprint. O(log n) membership proofs — 20 hashes verify 1 record in 1M. Key uses: DynamoDB/Cassandra anti-entropy repair (replicas exchange root hashes, walk tree to find diverged key ranges without full scans), Git (Merkle DAG for delta-transfer between repos), IPFS (content-addressing via CIDs where root hash = content identifier). Security: any leaf change propagates to root. To review: Verkle trees (Ethereum upgrading from Merkle), UnixFS v2 in IPFS, DynamoDB/Cassandra anti-entropy improvements.

Fully managed LLM evaluation service in Bedrock (GA March 2025). A judge LLM (Claude Sonnet 4, Llama 3.3, Mistral Large) scores (prompt, response) pairs from S3 using built-in metrics: Correctness, Completeness, Coherence, Harmfulness, AnswerRefusal. Returns normalized score 0-1 + rationale per example. Invoked via boto3 create_evaluation_job() or Bedrock console. Key use case: benchmark moderation models, catch prompt regressions, audit borderline cases. AgentCore Evaluations (Dec 2025) extends to agentic pipelines with 13 built-in evaluators and continuous online monitoring. Search at review: Amazon Bedrock Model Evaluation LLM-as-judge new features, AgentCore Evaluations updates.

LLM inference speedup technique: small draft model proposes γ tokens autoregressively, large target model verifies all in one parallel forward pass. Rejection sampling: accept token x̃ with prob min(1, p_target/p_draft); on rejection sample residual distribution norm(max(0, p-q)). Expected tokens/step = (1 - α^(γ+1))/(1-α) where α=mean acceptance rate. Real-world 2-3x speedup at zero quality loss. Production: vLLM --speculative-model flag, TensorRT-LLM, AWS Bedrock Nova models. Tree-based variants (TETRIS 2025) extend chain to tree for higher acceptance. Search: speculative decoding LLM 2026 acceptance rate production deployment AWS Bedrock

topic: Stanford AI Index 2026: The Year AI Became Undeniable
context_for_review: Annual Stanford HAI report (9th edition, April 2026). Key stats: SWE-bench coding benchmark 60%→~100% in 1 year; inference costs ~$30/M tokens (2023)→<$1/M (2026), ~10x reduction/year; 88% org adoption (up from 78%); entry-level software dev jobs down ~20% for ages 22-25 (first measurable white-collar AI displacement); US $285.9B vs China $12.4B private AI investment but capability gap <3%; Gemini gold medal at IMO 2025; models now exceed human expert baseline on PhD-level science; $172B annual consumer value from gen AI. At review time search: 'Stanford AI Index 2027 key findings', 'AI inference cost 2027', 'entry level developer jobs AI impact 2027'.

DPO eliminates the reward model from RLHF by reparameterising the optimal policy as an implicit reward. Loss: -E[log sigma(beta*log(pi_theta(y_w|x)/pi_ref(y_w|x)) - beta*log(pi_theta(y_l|x)/pi_ref(y_l|x)))]. Requires preference pairs (prompt, chosen, rejected) + frozen reference model (usually SFT checkpoint). beta controls deviation from reference. Key 2025 variants: SimPO (length-normalised, no ref model), ORPO (single-stage SFT+alignment), KTO (unpaired data), GRPO (used in DeepSeek-R1 reasoning). At review: check which variant dominates at scale, new Bedrock fine-tuning support, and whether DPO has been superseded for content moderation use cases.

MoE replaces transformer FFN layers with N expert FFNs + a learned router (softmax over TopK logits). Only K experts activate per token. Load balancing via auxiliary loss (L=alpha*N*sum(f_i*P_i)) or DeepSeek-V3's auxiliary-loss-free dynamic bias. Key models: Mixtral 8x7B (8 experts, top-2, 46.7B total/12.9B active), DeepSeek-V3 (256 experts, top-8, 671B/37B), Llama 4 Scout (16 experts, top-1), Qwen3-235B (128 experts, top-8, 235B/22B). Memory-hungry (all experts in VRAM) but compute-efficient. Fine-tuning risk: expert collapse under narrow distributions. Bedrock hosts Mixtral. Search for: new MoE models released, routing innovations, expert collapse mitigation techniques.

IO-aware exact attention algorithm by Dao et al. (2022); tiling + online softmax avoid materialising N×N score matrix in HBM; memory drops O(N²)→O(N), enables 8K–128K+ context on same hardware; versions FA1 (2022), FA2 (2023, seq-parallel), FA3 (2024, H100 async), FA4 (2025, kernel fusion); default backend in vLLM, SGLang, TGI, PyTorch SDPA; check for FA5 or new Hopper/Blackwell optimisations; relevant to Bedrock/SageMaker inference sizing and long-doc content moderation

4-module course: types (covariate/label/concept/feature drift), detection methods (KS, chi-squared, PSI, JSD, MMD, classifier-based), production monitoring (SageMaker Model Monitor, Evidently AI, Alibi Detect, CloudWatch alerting), response strategies (scheduled/triggered retraining, sliding window, exponential weighting, replay buffer, shadow mode). PSI thresholds: <0.1 stable, 0.1-0.2 moderate, >0.2 major. At review: check for new SageMaker Model Monitor features, Evidently AI updates, and whether continuous learning approaches have matured for transformer-based moderation models.

Maps both servers and keys onto a virtual hash ring; key belongs to next server clockwise. Adding/removing a node only reshuffles ~1/N keys vs nearly all with naive modulo hashing. Virtual nodes (vnodes): each physical server gets multiple ring positions for even load distribution (Cassandra uses 256 vnodes). Original paper: Karger et al. MIT 1997; popularised by Amazon Dynamo 2007. Used in: Cassandra, DynamoDB, Redis Cluster, Akamai CDN. Search angle at review: check for new distributed DB or caching systems adopting/dropping consistent hashing, and any vnodes improvements.

Sim-to-real transfer bridges the gap between simulation and reality for robot policies. Three techniques: (1) Domain Randomization — randomize physics/visual params (mass, friction, lighting, texture) so real world is just another variant; (2) System Identification — carefully measure real hardware and tune sim to match; (3) Adaptive/Meta-Learning — policy learns to calibrate itself from short real-world bursts. Sim-to-real gap has 3 layers: physics gap (friction/damping), perception gap (camera/lighting), actuator gap (backlash/drift). NVIDIA Newton 1.0 released at GTC 2026 — open-source GPU-accelerated physics engine by NVIDIA+Google DeepMind+Disney Research, 475x faster than MJX, handles deformables and hydroelastic contact. Full NVIDIA pipeline: Isaac Sim + Isaac Lab + Newton + Cosmos world models. Unitree robots performed autonomous kung fu at China Spring Festival Gala Feb 2026, trained entirely in sim. At review: check Newton adoption, new humanoid deployments, real2sim2real (ReBot) progress.

Two flavours: CvRDT (state-based, share whole state, merge by taking least upper bound) and CmRDT (operation-based, share ops that commute in any order). Core requirement: merge must be commutative, associative, idempotent. G-Counter is simplest CRDT: per-node slots, merge by taking max of each slot. Sequence CRDTs (Yjs, Automerge) assign unique IDs to every character for deterministic merge regardless of arrival order. Real-world use: Figma, Notion, Riak, Redis geo-replication. vs OT (Google Docs): OT needs central server to sequence ops, CRDTs are decentralised. At review: check Yjs/Automerge new releases, local-first software adoption, any AWS DynamoDB CRDT features, Figma/Notion architecture posts.

Two main strategies: sequential scaling (long Chain-of-Thought CoT - model reasons through thousands of tokens before answering, used by o1/o3/DeepSeek-R1) and parallel scaling (Best-of-N sampling, beam search, MCTS). Key enabler: Process Reward Models (PRMs) score intermediate reasoning steps vs Outcome Reward Models that only score final answers. DeepSeek-R1 trained via pure RL on hard problems - reasoning emerges without step-level supervision. Adaptive compute: route easy queries to fast models, hard cases to reasoning models. Inference will exceed training compute by 118x by 2026. At review time search: 'test-time compute scaling 2027 latest models', 'process reward models advances 2027', 'OpenAI o3 o4 reasoning inference cost', 'DeepSeek R2 reasoning capabilities'.

topic: Google NotebookLM — Your AI Research Brain
context_for_review: AI research assistant grounded on user-uploaded sources only (PDFs, Docs, URLs, YouTube). Signature feature: Audio Overviews — two AI hosts generate a podcast from your documents, 80+ languages, four formats (Deep Dive, Brief, Critique, Debate). June 2026 update: upgraded to Gemini 3.5, in-notebook Python code execution via cloud computer, agentic source discovery using Google Search, new output formats (charts, spreadsheets, PDFs, PowerPoint, Cinematic Video Overviews). Free tier useful; AI Ultra unlocks full features. Best used as project-scoped second brain. At review time search: NotebookLM new features 2026, pricing updates, Gemini upgrades, competitor tools.

WBC = coordinating all robot joints simultaneously (30-50 joints) so limbs are coupled — reaching affects balance. Traditional approach: real-time physics optimisation 200x/sec. Modern: RL in simulation (PPO), deploy a fast neural network policy. Behaviour Foundation Models (BFMs) pre-train transferable body skills at scale. Key 2026 trend: BFMs as the robotics equivalent of pre-trained LLMs. Central bottleneck for humanoid deployment. Search: 'whole-body control humanoid 2026', 'behavior foundation models robotics', arxiv 2506.20487.

LoRA freezes pretrained weights and learns two small matrices A (d×r) and B (r×d) per transformer layer; effective weight = W + B·A, only A and B trained. Rank r is key param: r=8-16 sweet spot, higher approaches full FT. Alpha/r scale factor decouples step size from rank. Applied to Q/K/V/O attention projections. At inference: B·A folds into W, zero overhead. QLoRA adds 4-bit NF4 quantization of base model (bfloat16 adapters), enabling 33B on 24GB GPU. DoRA (ICML 2024) decomposes weight into magnitude+direction, applies LoRA to direction only — outperforms LoRA at same rank. S-LoRA serves thousands of adapters per GPU cluster. Adapter merging: linearly combine B·A matrices from multiple adapters. At review: check DoRA adoption, LoRA variants (HiRA, SSMLoRA), Unsloth speed benchmarks, Bedrock fine-tuning updates, PEFT library releases.

topic: SpaceX Buys Cursor — When a Rocket Company Buys a Code Editor
context_for_review: June 16, 2026: SpaceX agreed to buy Anysphere (maker of Cursor) for $60B all-stock, largest-ever VC-backed startup acquisition. SpaceX (not xAI) is the buyer because it just IPO'd June 12 ($75B raised, largest IPO ever, $2.7T market cap) and has stock to spend; xAI lost all 11 co-founders by end of March 2026 and needs talent/compute. Deal gets xAI Cursor's coding data + engineers; Cursor gets access to xAI's Colossus supercompute; joint model planned for Cursor + Grok Build. Option signed April 21 2026 with ~$10B walk-away fee, well before the IPO. Closing expected Q3 2026 pending regulatory approval. Pattern: 2026 AI M&A (Qualcomm/Modular $4B, OpenAI/Ona-Gitpod) increasingly buys data+compute+talent over product. Governance angle: cross-company deals within Musk's ecosystem (SpaceX/xAI) raise conflict-of-interest questions for new SpaceX public shareholders. At review: check deal closing status/regulatory approval, whether the joint Cursor+Grok Build model shipped, Cursor product changes post-acquisition, any shareholder/antitrust pushback, further Musk-company consolidation.

topic: Raft Consensus — How Distributed Systems Agree on One Truth
context_for_review: Raft: consensus algorithm by Ongaro/Ousterhout (Stanford), designed to be more understandable than Paxos. Servers are Follower/Candidate/Leader. Randomized election timeouts trigger elections; RequestVote RPC; majority vote wins leadership for a 'term'. Leader replicates log via AppendEntries (also used as heartbeat); entry is 'committed' once stored on a majority of servers. Used in production: etcd (Kubernetes control plane), Kafka KRaft mode (replaced ZooKeeper), CockroachDB, Consul, HashiCorp tools. At review time: check for updates to Kafka KRaft adoption, any new Raft variants/optimizations (e.g. Flexible Paxos comparisons), or newer consensus algorithms gaining traction (e.g. for AI infra / distributed training coordination).

GQA groups query heads to share K/V projections, cutting KV-cache memory (Llama 3 70B: 64 query heads -> 8 KV heads, 8x reduction) with near-MHA quality. Spectrum: MHA (1:1) -> GQA (group:1) -> MQA (all:1). Inference is memory-bandwidth bound so smaller KV cache = faster decode + bigger batches. Standard by 2026 in Llama 3/4, Mistral, Qwen, Gemma. DeepSeek V2/V3/R1 instead use Multi-Head Latent Attention (MLA), which compresses K/V into a learned latent space (~7x smaller than GQA) rather than sharing heads. Relevant to Bedrock/Nova/SageMaker self-hosted serving (num_key_value_heads config lever). At review time search: 'MLA vs GQA adoption 2027', 'new KV cache compression techniques 2027', 'attention mechanism efficiency 2027 frontier models'.

As of Claude Code v2.1.172 (week of June 8-12, 2026), subagents can spawn their own subagents (not just the main conversation). Nesting capped at 5 levels below main, hard-coded/not configurable; a depth-5 subagent isn't given the Agent tool. Enable by listing Agent in a subagent's tools: frontmatter; omit or disallowedTools to block. Intended pattern: fan-out-within-delegation (e.g. reviewer spawns one verifier per finding), only top-level summary returns to main. Background subagent depth is fixed at spawn time, resuming doesn't change it. Source: official docs code.claude.com/docs/en/sub-agents#spawn-nested-subagents and whats-new/2026-w24. At review: check for depth-limit changes, new nested-subagent patterns/examples, cost-tracking tools for nested trees, whats-new digests after week 24.

Mamba/SSMs replace attention's growing lookup with a fixed-size 'state' that updates per-token (linear time, constant memory) vs transformer's quadratic attention. Selective SSM = update/forget gates depend on input content (unlike fixed-dynamics S4). Mamba-2 linked SSM recurrence to attention math (structured state space duality); Mamba-3 (ICLR 2026) uses complex-valued state updates for more expressiveness. Tradeoff: fixed-size state is lossy compression, so pure SSMs lag transformers on exact copying/retrieval of one specific past detail. 2026 trend: hybrids (Jamba interleaves Mamba blocks + attention blocks, sometimes with MoE) dominate production rather than pure SSMs. At review time: check for new Mamba-3 adoption, any Bedrock/SageMaker hybrid-SSM model releases, updates to Jamba or similar hybrid models, and any changes in the SSM-vs-transformer long-context benchmark results (arXiv 2507.12442 was the key 2026 benchmark).

MLA (DeepSeek-V2/V3/R1) compresses K/V for ALL heads into one shared low-rank latent vector per token (~512 dims) instead of per-head (MHA) or per-group (GQA) copies, cutting KV cache ~5-13x smaller than GQA and 90%+ smaller than MHA. Per-head K/V reconstructed via head-specific up-projection (decoder lens); the up-projection can be mathematically absorbed into the query projection so inference never fully decompresses. RoPE doesn't survive compression cleanly, so DeepSeek uses 'decoupled RoPE': a small uncompressed positional side-channel cached separately alongside the compressed content latent. Unlike GQA/MQA, DeepSeek reports MLA slightly outperforms full MHA despite the much smaller cache. This is why DeepSeek-V3/R1 (deployable via SageMaker JumpStart/Bedrock custom import) can serve 128K context affordably. At review time search: 'MLA adoption other model families 2027', 'multi-head latent attention variants 2027', 'DeepSeek V4 architecture', 'KV cache compression techniques 2027'.

June 29, 2026: California signed first-of-its-kind statewide AI deal with Anthropic — all state agencies + opt-in cities/counties get Claude at 50% discount via new SITeS marketplace (Statewide IT Shared Services). DMV uses it for customer service, Dept of Health Care Services for workflows, Dept of Technology + Governor's Office of Emergency Services use Claude/Claude Code for cybersecurity. Parallel in-house assistant 'Poppy' piloted with 2800+ employees across 67 depts, statewide rollout July 2026. At review: check SITeS adoption numbers, whether other states follow California's model, Poppy statewide rollout results, any security incidents or public pushback.

RoPE rotates query/key vector pairs by a position-dependent angle so attention naturally depends on relative token distance, not absolute position. Different dimension-pairs rotate at different frequencies (fast=local order, slow=long-range structure). Default in LLaMA/Mistral/Qwen/DeepSeek; spreading to encoders like ModernBERT. Context extension beyond training length needs frequency rescaling: Position Interpolation, NTK-aware scaling, YaRN (combines NTK-by-parts + attention temperature, standard for 8K->128K+). Known 2026 limitation: scaling fixes perplexity/fluency but not full retrieval -- 'lost in the middle' persists for facts buried deep in very long contexts. Review at future date: check if newer context-extension methods (post-YaRN) have become standard, and whether 'lost in the middle' has been meaningfully fixed in 2026-2027 model releases.

topic: ChatGPT Work — OpenAI's All-in-One Agent Desktop
context_for_review: July 9, 2026: OpenAI merged Codex, a new agentic 'Work' mode, and the standalone Atlas browser into one unified ChatGPT desktop app (Mac/Windows, free for all users), powered by GPT-5.6. Three tabs: Chat, Work, Codex. Work connects to Slack, Teams, Google Drive, SharePoint, email, calendars, CRMs, project trackers via plugins and returns finished artifacts (decks, sheets, docs, small web apps) instead of just chat replies. Atlas browser discontinued as standalone product; agentic-browsing moves into desktop app's built-in browser pane + updated Chrome sidebar extension. GPT-5.6 adds stronger computer-use ability: inspects rendered output (not just generated code/text) to catch visual/functional issues before returning work. Rollout: Pro/Enterprise/Edu first, Plus/Business within days; unified desktop app itself free immediately, existing Codex installs auto-upgrade. Positioned as direct competitor to Claude Cowork. At review: check ChatGPT Work adoption/reception, feature parity vs Claude Cowork, GPT-5.6 benchmark results, whether connector ecosystem grows, any incidents from agentic browsing inside the desktop app.

VLA models: single network mapping camera + language instruction -> continuous robot actions, built by fine-tuning a pretrained vision-language backbone with an action-output head. Physical Intelligence pi0 uses PaliGemma (3B VLM) + flow-matching action expert (~50Hz continuous control, diffusion-style refinement instead of token-by-token prediction). Same base model shown driving humanoid, wheeled manipulator, and fixed arm without per-robot retraining. Key 2026 players: NVIDIA GR00T N1, Figure AI Helix, Physical Intelligence pi0/pi0-FAST. Field shifting toward 'world models' that predict action consequences before acting. At review time search: 'VLA model progress 2027', 'humanoid robot foundation model deployment 2027', 'world models robotics 2027'.
